title: Electrophysiology FEM Simulation with openCARP
description: Finite-element simulation of electrical wave propagation in cardiac tissue and organ-scale geometries using openCARP.

details.overview: Simulating the electrical activity of cardiac tissue at tissue and organ scale using the Finite Element Method. The model resolves propagation of action potentials across patient-derived geometries, enabling the study of how structural heterogeneities and ion channel mutations contribute to arrhythmia.
details.approach: Built on the openCARP framework. Meshes derived from clinical imaging data. Ionic models (ten Tusscher–Panfilov) coupled with bidomain/monodomain PDEs solved via FEM. Parallelised across HPC cluster nodes.
details.outcomes: In progress — part of Final Year Project.

title: Physics-Informed Neural Operator for EP Surrogate Modelling
description: A physics-informed neural operator as a surrogate model for cardiac electrophysiology simulations, enabling fast inference while respecting PDE constraints.

details.overview: Training a neural operator to serve as a real-time surrogate for the FEM-based EP simulator. The goal is to replace expensive PDE solves with a learned mapping that preserves the underlying physics.
details.approach: Physics-informed loss combining data-driven residual minimisation with PDE residuals. Architecture based on neural operator frameworks. Training data generated from the openCARP simulator.
details.outcomes: In progress — part of Final Year Project.

title: Biomedical Imaging with AI
description: End-to-end pipeline for medical image analysis from raw data preprocessing to deformable image registration using deep learning.

details.overview: End-to-end pipeline for medical image analysis, developed as a course project. Covers the full workflow from raw DICOM data to registered output volumes.
details.approach: Preprocessing: noise reduction, intensity normalisation, bias field correction. Segmentation and feature extraction. Deformable image registration using deep learning.
details.outcomes: Completed as part of coursework. Pipeline tested on publicly available medical imaging datasets.

title: CPU Design from NAND Gates
description: Full CPU built from first principles: NAND gates to logic gates to ALU to instruction set, implemented in HDL.

details.overview: Designed and implemented a functional 16-bit CPU from first principles, starting from a single NAND gate primitive. Completed as part of a computer organisation course.
details.approach: Bottom-up construction: NAND → basic gates → multiplexers → ALU → registers → program counter → CPU. Described entirely in HDL and verified in a hardware simulator.
details.outcomes: Completed. Full instruction set implemented and tested with assembly programs.

title: Protein Characterization & Gene Editing — Temasek Life Sciences Laboratory
description: Summer research internship in plant genetics. Protein characterization and gene editing workflows including plasmid synthesis and transfection.

details.overview: Summer research internship in the plant genetics group at Temasek Life Sciences Laboratory (TLL), Singapore. Focused on characterising proteins involved in plant stress response pathways.
details.approach: Protein expression and purification workflows. Characterisation via SDS-PAGE and electron microscopy. Gene editing pipeline: plasmid design, synthesis, and transfection into plant cell lines. PCR-based validation of editing outcomes.
details.outcomes: Completed Summer 2024. Contributed to ongoing research on plant stress-response mechanisms.
